page_content: HNUH269P Building Community: How to Make Friends (3 Credits) In 2017, U.S. Surgeon General Vivek Murthy deemed loneliness an "epidemic." While the rise of social media is meant to foster connection, over 23% of adults report being lonely and social networks have been shrinking for decades. Despite increasing rates of loneliness, it is still possible to overcome these trends and ﬁnd connection. We tend to assume that we should know how to connect with others intuitively, but, as rising rates of loneliness indicate, this is not the case: connection is something we must learn and practice. In this applied course, students will learn the science of connection and engage in practical activities designed to help them make and keep friends. They will leave the course better able to foster meaningful connections. This course is self- contained but paired with HNUH269T in the Building Community track, where you will learn the social value of showing up, for the world and for yourself. Credit Only Granted for: HNUH248U or HNUH269P. Formerly: HNUH248U.
